top_5_citable.heading: Resume Keyword Optimization
top_5_citable.label: Low Citability
top_5_citable.preview: Use this advanced process to improve ATS keyword match while keeping your resume clear, credible, and human-readable. Keyword optimization is one of the highest-leverage steps in resume improvement. ATS systems...

top_5_citable.heading: Keyword rewrite example
top_5_citable.label: Low Citability
top_5_citable.preview: After:"Planned and optimized multi-channel demand generation campaigns, increasing MQL-to-SQL conversion by 22% in two quarters." This version includes role-relevant keywords (demand generation, multi-channel, conversion) plus measurable outcome.

top_5_citable.heading: FAQ: Resume keyword optimization
top_5_citable.label: Low Citability
top_5_citable.preview: How many ATS keywords should a resume include?There is no fixed number. Include the highest-priority terms naturally where your experience supports them. Can keyword optimization increase interview chances?Yes. Better role...

top_5_citable.heading: Where to place keywords for maximum clarity
top_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
top_5_citable.preview: A practical placement framework that balances ATS and human reading: Headline/Summary:role identity + 2-3 critical domain terms.Skills block:grouped tools and methods by relevance, not alphabetical order.Experience bullets:context + keyword +...

top_5_citable.heading: Step 3: Place keywords strategically
top_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
top_5_citable.preview: Place primary keywords in three zones: headline/summary, skills section, and recent impact bullets. Do not rely on a single keyword dump list. Distribution across meaningful sections increases both ATS relevance...

bottom_5_citable.heading: Step 1: Extract role-critical terms
bottom_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
bottom_5_citable.preview: Read the target job post and capture repeated phrases, required tools, responsibilities, and domain-specific terms. Separate "must-have" terms from "nice-to-have" terms. This creates a clear keyword hierarchy before editing your...

bottom_5_citable.heading: Step 2: Map terms to real experience
bottom_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
bottom_5_citable.preview: Only use keywords supported by real experience. For each important term, identify one bullet where you can show context, action, and result. This keeps the resume honest and improves interview...

bottom_5_citable.heading: Step 4: Validate and iterate
bottom_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
bottom_5_citable.preview: Run your resume in theATS checker, review missing or weak terms, and iterate. If your score improves but bullets sound vague, refine with theATS mistakes guide.

bottom_5_citable.heading: How to prioritize keywords correctly
bottom_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
bottom_5_citable.preview: Priority 1:Core role and mandatory skills (hard requirements).Priority 2:Tool stack and workflow terms used repeatedly in the vacancy.Priority 3:Domain language, stakeholder context, and scale indicators.Priority 4:Adjacent terms that support, but...

bottom_5_citable.heading: Common keyword optimization mistakes
bottom_5_citable.label: Poor Citability
bottom_5_citable.preview: Copying long chunks from the job post without evidence.Using broad buzzwords with no project-level context.Ignoring section placement and relying only on a skills list.Optimizing for ATS only and weakening human...
